title: jasminum grandiflorum subsp. floribundum
intro: Jasminum grandiflorum subsp. floribundum, also known as jasmine or chameli, is a subspecies of the Jasminum grandiflorum plant, a flowering evergreen shrub native to India and Sri Lanka. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are derived from the essential oils found in its flowers. The health benefits of Jasminum grandiflorum subsp. floribundum include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Jasminum grandiflorum subsp. floribundum include teas, essential oils, and extracts, which are often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
